name: Double-Decker Fudge
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Hershey's semi-sweet chocolate chips or HERSHEY'S MINI CHIPS - semi-sweet chocolate
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8-inch square pan with foil, extending foil over the edges of the pan.
2. Measure peanut butter chips into one medium bowl and chocolate chips into a second medium bowl.
3. In a heavy 3-quart saucepan, combine sugar, marshmallow creme, evaporated milk, and butter.
4. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils. Continue boiling and stirring for an additional 5 minutes.
5. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla extract.
6. Stir in half of the hot mixture (1 1/2 cups) into peanut butter chips until the chips are completely melted. Quickly pour into the prepared pan.
7. Stir the remaining half of the hot mixture into chocolate chips until the chips are completely melted. Spread over the top of the peanut butter layer.
8. Cool; refrigerate for 1 1/2 hours or until firm.
9. Cut into 1-inch squares. Store tightly covered in the refrigerator. Makes about 60 pieces or about 2 pounds candy.
